title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen and Stephen Megalas discuss the significance of the "god of the sea" pipe found in the gazebo. Ellery initially theorizes that the killer, Crosetti, left the pipe there to falsely imply that Thomas Braid was smoking in the gazebo willingly, suggesting the gazebo was not the actual crime scene. Ellery proposes that the real crime occurred in Braid's study, where Braid was last seen alive.

Megalas corrects Ellery, noting that Crosetti must have known the pipe belonged to Megalas because it was kept in a uniquely shaped box engraved with Megalas's full name in gold leaf. Ellery concludes that Crosetti deliberately left the pipe to create a temporary illusion that it belonged to Braid, but stole the distinctive box to prevent the mistake from being immediately discovered. Ellery reasons that Crosetti's actions are designed to lead investigators toward the real crime scene now, because Megalas's return will inevitably reveal the truth about the missing box.

Following this logic, Ellery leads the group to Braid's study, which has been closed since the discovery of Braid's body. During a thorough search of the room, Ellery moves a sofa away from the wall and discovers a dried black bloodstain on the floor. He notices a square indentation in the carpet fibers near the stain that does not match the rounded feet of the sofa. After moving the sofa further, he finds a matching indentation three feet away, leading Ellery to conclude that the bloodstain was intentionally hidden by moving the carpet.
